title: andropogon nardus subsp. marginatus
intro: Andropogon nardus subsp. marginatus, also known as Sea Spike or Sea Nard, is a type of grass native to the coastal regions of Australia, characterized by its distinctive yellowish-green leaves and tall, slender stalks. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which make it effective in treating wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Sea Spike include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and alleviate symptoms of colds, coughs, and sore throats. In herbal preparations, Sea Spike is often used in the form of teas, infusions, or tinctures, and can also be applied topically as a compress or poultice to aid in the healing process.
